Title: Validate a payment request using OTP
Content:
Post
/payment_requests/{payment_request_id}/validate_otp

Submit OTP to validate the payment request that was created. In supported payment channels with flows that require OTP, merchants have the option to submit the OTP via API. Merchants should build a page to receive OTP input from end user when they receive the OTP notification.

Security
HTTP
Type basic
Path parameters
payment_request_id
stringRequired
Min length39
Max length39
Examplepr-8877c08a-740d-4153-9816-3d744ed197a5
Body parameters
otp_code
string

OTP code used to verify the transaction with payment method provider.

Example123456
Responses
200

Validate OTP for a Payment Request

validateOtpPaymentResponseRedirect
{
  "business_id": "5f27a14a9bf05c73dd040bc8",
  "reference_id": "90392f42-d98a-49ef-a7f3-abcdefu",
  "payment_request_id": "pr-90392f42-d98a-49ef-a7f3-abcdefu",
  "customer_id": "cust-90392f42-d98a-49ef-a7f3-abcdefu",
  "type": "SINGLE_PAYMENT",
  "country": "ID",
  "currency": "IDR",
  "request_amount": 10000,
  "capture_method": "AUTOMATIC",
  "channel_code": "DANA",
  "channel_properties": {
    "failure_return_url": "https://xendit.co/badstuff",
    "success_return_url": "https://xendit.co/goodstuff"
  },
  "actions": [
    {
      "type": "API_POST_REQUEST",
      "descriptor": "VALIDATE_OTP",
      "value": "https://api.xendit.co/v3/payment_requests/pr-2681d003-612c-49a8-95b2-fd97bc8e8790/validate_otp"
    },
    {
      "type": "API_POST_REQUEST",
      "descriptor": "RESEND_OTP",
      "value": "https://api.xendit.co/v3/payment_requests/pr-2681d003-612c-49a8-95b2-fd97bc8e8790/send_otp"
    }
  ],
  "status": "REQUIRES_ACTION",
  "description": "Greedisgood",
  "metadata": {
    "metametadata": "metaception"
  },
  "shipping_information": {
    "city": "Singapore",
    "country": "SG",
    "postal_code": "666999",
    "street_line1": "Marina Bay Sands Suites",
    "street_line2": "21-37",
    "province_state": "Singapore"
  },
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "PHYSICAL_PRODUCT",
      "name": "Dyson Vacuum",
      "net_unit_amount": 10000,
      "quantity": 1
    }
  ],
  "created": "2021-12-31T11:59:59Z",
  "updated": "2021-12-31T11:59:59Z"
}

object

Payment request object

business_id
string

Xendit-generated identifier for the business that owns the transaction

Example5f27a14a9bf05c73dd040bc8
reference_id
string

A Reference ID from merchants to identify their request.

Min length1
Max length64
payment_request_id
string

Xendit unique Payment Request ID generated as reference after creation of payment request.

Examplepr-1102feb0-bb79-47ae-9d1e-e69394d3949c
payment_token_id
string

Xendit unique Payment Token ID generated as reference for reusable payment details of the end user.

Examplept-cc3938dc-c2a5-43c4-89d7-7570793348c2
customer_id
string

Xendit unique Capture ID generated as reference for the end user

Min length41
Max length41
Examplecust-b98d6f63-d240-44ec-9bd5-aa42954c4f48
latest_payment_id
string

Latest Payment ID linked to the payment request.

Examplepy-1402feb0-bb79-47ae-9d1e-e69394d3949c
type
string

The payment collection intent type for the payment request. SINGLE_PAYMENT: receive a single payment for the request. multiple captures are encapsulated as a single payment. individual recurring payments counts as a single payment each. PAY_AND_SAVE: receive a single payment for the request. if the payment is successful, a payment token will be returned for subsequent use. MULTIPLE_PAYMENTS: receive multiple payments for the request. only used for fixed payment instruments like static QR or fixed VAs etc.

Valid values[ "SINGLE_PAYMENT", "PAY_AND_SAVE", "MULTIPLE_PAYMENTS" ]
country
string

ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 two-letter country code for the country of transaction.

Valid values[ "ID", "PH", "VN", "TH", "SG", "MY" ]
ExampleID
currency
string

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment.

Valid values[ "IDR", "PHP", "VND", "THB", "SGD", "MYR", "USD" ]
ExampleIDR
request_amount
number

The intended payment amount to be collected from the end user.

Minimum0
Example10000
capture_method
string

AUTOMATIC: payment capture will be processed immediately after payment request is created. MANUAL: payment capture requires merchant's trigger via payment capture endpoint before being processed

Valid values[ "AUTOMATIC", "MANUAL" ]
Default"AUTOMATIC"
ExampleAUTOMATIC
channel_code
string
channel_properties
OneOf
ChannelPropertiesCards
mid_label
string

For Switcher Merchants only. Specify the MID label to route the payment.

statement_descriptor
string

Short descriptor that can be used as an identifier on the end user card statement. Subjected to support by issuers.

Min length41
Max length41
skip_three_ds
boolean

Specify if 3DS should be skipped for the payment.

Defaultfalse
success_return_url
string

URL where the end-customer is redirected if the linking is successful. This is required for skip 3DS transactions to handle soft declines.

Min length41
Max length41
failure_return_url
string

URL where the end-customer is redirected if the linking has failed. This is required for skip 3DS transactions to handle soft declines.

Min length41
Max length41
card_on_file_type
string

Required when creating payment tokens or when payment request type is PAY_AND_SAVE:. The type of “credential-on-file” / “card-on-file” / COF for future payments.

If you intend to use the card details for future COF transactions, you must be accurately indicate the card_on_file_type to allow Xendit to optimize the payment setup with the processor, which will help all future transactions using the same card details.

CUSTOMER_UNSCHEDULED: for future payments initiated by the customer that do not follow a schedule. For example, a simple “save card for future checkout” eCommerce flow, where the customer can use the saved card for future purchases any time.

MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED: for future payments initiated without customer interaction and do not follow a schedule. For example, an auto top-up payment flow, where an application automatically collects payment when customer's balance is low.

RECURRING: for future payments initiated at fixed and regular intervals. For example, a subscription payment flow, where the customer is billed automatically every month.

To process MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED and RECURRING payments, there are some additional configuration required. For Aggregator Merchants, you need to be allowed to perform transaction without 3DS, please check our guides to enable this, or contact a Xendit representative for help. For Switcher Merchants, please check with your acquiring bank whether your MID allows non 3DS transactions and support recurring transactions by the acquirer.

Valid values[ "CUSTOMER_UNSCHEDULED", "MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED", "RECURRING" ]
card_details
object

Information about the card that is required to process card transactions.

type
string

Type of card used for the transaction.

Valid values[ "DEBIT", "CREDIT" ]
issuer
string

Issuing party of the end user card.

country
string

2-letter ISO 3166-2 country code for the country where the card was issued.

Min length2
Max length2
network
string

Name of the card scheme.

fingerprint
string

Unique consistent identifier for the card. This does not change values over different authorizations.

masked_card_number
string

Masked card number for the submitted physical or virtual card number.

card_number
string

Card number as displayed on the physical or virtual card.

Min length13
Max length19
cardholder_first_name
string

Cardholder name as displayed on the physical or virtual card.

Min length1
Max length100
cardholder_last_name
string

Cardholder name as displayed on the physical or virtual card.

Min length1
Max length100
expiry_month
string

Card expiry month as displayed on the physical or virtual card.

Min length2
Max length2
expiry_year
string

Card expiry year as displayed on the physical or virtual card.

Min length4
Max length4
cvn
string

Card verification number as displayed on the physical or virtual card.

Min length3
Max length4
Pattern^[0-9]+$
cardholder_email
string (email)

Cardholder email as registered with issuing bank.

Min length5
Max length254
cardholder_phone_number
string

Cardholder phone number as registered with issuing bank.

Min length7
Max length15
authentication_data
object
flow
string

Type of 3DS authentication.

Valid values[ "FRICTIONLESS", "CHALLENGE" ]
a_res
object

The issuer response to the 3DS request.

eci
string

Payment system-specific value provided by the ACS or DS to indicate the results of the attempt to authenticate the end user.

directory_server_trans_id
string

Universally unique transaction identifier assigned by the 3DS server to identify a 3DS authentication.

authentication_value
string

The result value from the 3DS transaction received from the ACS. This value is no longer present on responses after 45 days have passed after the authentication. Note that Mastercard and Visa use a different underlying format. This property will only be present in the ARes if transStatus is Y, A and conditionally (by DS rules) I.

message_version
string

The 3DS protocol version which has been used to perform 3DS.

Valid values[ "2.1.0", "2.2.0" ]
authorization_data
object
network_response_code
string

Network response code return by card scheme which indicates the status of the payment processing.

Min length2
Max length2
network_response_code_descriptor
string

Detailed description of the network response code that was returned.

Min length6969
Max length6969
cvn_verification_result
string

The result of cvn verification. M - match, N - no match

Valid values[ "M", "N" ]
address_verification_result
string

The result of billing address verification. M - match, N - no match

Valid values[ "M", "N" ]
authorization_code
string

Authorization code as received from the card network.

Min length6
Max length6
retrieval_reference_number
string

Transaction identifier used for identifying transactions between issuer/ acquirer/ end user. This field is often displayed in the statement or receipt of the end user.

Min length6969
Max length6969
acquirer_merchant_id
string

Merchant ID as configured with the acquirer. This field is only shown when Xendit merchant is on switching model.

Min length6969
Max length6969
reconciliation_id
string

Transaction identifier used in reconciliation statements or reports provided by acquirer. This field is only shown when Xendit merchant is on switching model.

Min length6969
Max length6969
network_transaction_id
string

Transaction identifier as received from the card network. This field is only shown when Xendit merchant is on switching model.

Min length6969
Max length6969
billing_information
object

Billing address used to verify identity of the end user. This should be passed in for US, UK, CA cards.

country

2-letter ISO 3166-2 country code for the customer’s shipping country

Valid values[ "ID", "PH", "VN", "TH", "SG", "MY" ]
street_line1
string

Building name and apartment unit number

Min length1
Max length50
street_line2
string

Building street address

Min length1
Max length50
city
string

City, village or town as appropriate

Min length1
Max length50
province_state
string

Either one of (whichever is applicable): Geographic area, province, or region / Formal state designation within country

Min length1
Max length50
postal_code
string

Postal, zip or rural delivery code, if applicable

Min length1
Max length50
installment_configuration
object

Installment configuration that will be used to process the payment.

terms
integer

The number of times the installment will be collected from the end user based on the interval

interval
string

The time interval between each installment payment.

Valid values[ "MONTH" ]
recurring_configuration
object

The recurring configuration that will used to process the payment. Required when card_on_file_type is RECURRING

recurring_expiry
string

The date at which the recurring payments expires. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

Min length10
Max length10
recurring_frequency
integer

Number of days between recurring payments.

Min length1
Max length2
ChannelPropertiesRedirection
success_return_url
string

URL where the end-customer is redirected if the transaction is successful.

Min length11
Max length41
failure_return_url
string

URL where the end-customer is redirected if the transaction has failed.

Min length11
Max length41
pending_return_url
string

URL where the end-customer is redirected if the transaction is pending.

Min length11
Max length41
cancel_return_url
string

URL where the end-customer is redirected if the transaction has cancelled.

Min length11
Max length41
actions
Array of object (Actions)
object

Actions object contains possible next steps merchants can take to proceed with payment collection from end user

type

The type of action that merchant system will need to handle to complete payment.

Valid values[ "PRESENT_TO_CUSTOMER", "REDIRECT_CUSTOMER", "API_POST_REQUEST" ]
descriptor

The type of action that merchant system will need to handle to complete payment.

Valid values[ "CAPTURE_PAYMENT", "PAYMENT_CODE", "QR_STRING", "VIRTUAL_ACCOUNT_NUMBER", "WEB_URL", "DEEPLINK_URL", "VALIDATE_OTP", "RESEND_OTP" ]
value
string

The specific value that will be used by merchant to complete the action

status
string

Status of the payment request.

Valid values[ "ACCEPTING_PAYMENTS", "REQUIRES_ACTION", "AUTHORIZED", "CANCELED", "EXPIRED", "SUCCEEDED", "FAILED" ]
ExampleSUCCEEDED
failure_code
string

Failure codes for payments.

Valid values[ "ACCOUNT_ACCESS_BLOCKED", "INVALID_MERCHANT_SETTINGS", "INVALID_ACCOUNT_DETAILS", "PAYMENT_ATTEMPT_COUNTS_EXCEEDED", "USER_DEVICE_UNREACHABLE", "CHANNEL_UNAVAILABLE", "INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE", "ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVATED", "INVALID_TOKEN", "SERVER_ERROR", "PARTNER_TIMEOUT_ERROR", "TIMEOUT_ERROR", "USER_DECLINED_PAYMENT", "USER_DID_NOT_AUTHORIZE", "PAYMENT_REQUEST_EXPIRED", "FAILURE_DETAILS_UNAVAILABLE", "EXPIRED_OTP", "INVALID_OTP", "PAYMENT_AMOUNT_LIMITS_EXCEEDED", "OTP_ATTEMPT_COUNTS_EXCEEDED", "CARD_DECLINED", "DECLINED_BY_ISSUER", "ISSUER_UNAVAILABLE", "INVALID_CVV", "DECLINED_BY_PROCESSOR", "CAPTURE_AMOUNT_EXCEEDED ", "AUTHENTICATION_FAILED" ]
ExampleCARD_DECLINED
description
string

A custom description for the Payment Request.

Min length1
Max length1000
ExamplePayment for your order #123
metadata
object (MerchantMetadata)

Key-value entries for your custom data. You can specify up to 50 keys, with key names up to 40 characters and values up to 500 characters. This is for your convenience. Xendit will not use this data for any processing.

Example{ "my_custom_id": "merchant-123", "my_custom_order_id": "order-123" }

Array of object (XenditStandardItem)

Array of objects describing the item/s attached to the payment.

Type of item

Valid values[ "DIGITAL_PRODUCTS", "PHYSICAL_PRODUCT", "DIGITAL_SERVICE", "PHYSICAL_SERVICE", "FEES - DISCOUNT" ]

integer

Number of units of this item in the basket

string

URL of the item. Must be HTTPS or HTTP

image_url
string

URL of the image of the item. Must be HTTPS or HTTP

string

string

Title: Validate a payment request using OTP
Content:
Post
/payment_requests/{payment_request_id}/validate_otp

Submit OTP to validate the payment request that was created. In supported payment channels with flows that require OTP, merchants have the option to submit the OTP via API. Merchants should build a page to receive OTP input from end user when they receive the OTP notification.

Security
HTTP
Type basic
Path parameters
payment_request_id
stringRequired
Min length39
Max length39
Examplepr-8877c08a-740d-4153-9816-3d744ed197a5
Body parameters
otp_code
string

OTP code used to verify the transaction with payment method provider.

Example123456
Responses
200

Validate OTP for a Payment Request

card_on_file_type
string

Required when creating payment tokens or when payment request type is PAY_AND_SAVE:. The type of “credential-on-file” / “card-on-file” / COF for future payments.

If you intend to use the card details for future COF transactions, you must be accurately indicate the card_on_file_type to allow Xendit to optimize the payment setup with the processor, which will help all future transactions using the same card details.

CUSTOMER_UNSCHEDULED: for future payments initiated by the customer that do not follow a schedule. For example, a simple “save card for future checkout” eCommerce flow, where the customer can use the saved card for future purchases any time.

MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED: for future payments initiated without customer interaction and do not follow a schedule. For example, an auto top-up payment flow, where an application automatically collects payment when customer's balance is low.

RECURRING: for future payments initiated at fixed and regular intervals. For example, a subscription payment flow, where the customer is billed automatically every month.

To process MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED and RECURRING payments, there are some additional configuration required. For Aggregator Merchants, you need to be allowed to perform transaction without 3DS, please check our guides to enable this, or contact a Xendit representative for help. For Switcher Merchants, please check with your acquiring bank whether your MID allows non 3DS transactions and support recurring transactions by the acquirer.

Valid values[ "CUSTOMER_UNSCHEDULED", "MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED", "RECURRING" ]

Title: Validate a payment request using OTP
Content:
Post
/payment_requests/{payment_request_id}/validate_otp

Submit OTP to validate the payment request that was created. In supported payment channels with flows that require OTP, merchants have the option to submit the OTP via API. Merchants should build a page to receive OTP input from end user when they receive the OTP notification.

Security
HTTP
Type basic
Path parameters
payment_request_id
stringRequired
Min length39
Max length39
Examplepr-8877c08a-740d-4153-9816-3d744ed197a5
Body parameters
otp_code
string

OTP code used to verify the transaction with payment method provider.

Example123456
Responses
200

Validate OTP for a Payment Request

validateOtpPaymentResponseRedirect
{
  "business_id": "5f27a14a9bf05c73dd040bc8",
  "reference_id": "90392f42-d98a-49ef-a7f3-abcdefu",
  "payment_request_id": "pr-90392f42-d98a-49ef-a7f3-abcdefu",
  "customer_id": "cust-90392f42-d98a-49ef-a7f3-abcdefu",
  "type": "SINGLE_PAYMENT",
  "country": "ID",
  "currency": "IDR",
  "request_amount": 10000,
  "capture_method": "AUTOMATIC",
  "channel_code": "DANA",
  "channel_properties": {
    "failure_return_url": "https://xendit.co/badstuff",
    "success_return_url": "https://xendit.co/goodstuff"
  },
  "actions": [
    {
      "type": "API_POST_REQUEST",
      "descriptor": "VALIDATE_OTP",
      "value": "https://api.xendit.co/v3/payment_requests/pr-2681d003-612c-49a8-95b2-fd97bc8e8790/validate_otp"
    },
    {
      "type": "API_POST_REQUEST",
      "descriptor": "RESEND_OTP",
      "value": "https://api.xendit.co/v3/payment_requests/pr-2681d003-612c-49a8-95b2-fd97bc8e8790/send_otp"
    }
  ],
  "status": "REQUIRES_ACTION",
  "description": "Greedisgood",
  "metadata": {
    "metametadata": "metaception"
  },
  "shipping_information": {
    "city": "Singapore",
    "country": "SG",
    "postal_code": "666999",
    "street_line1": "Marina Bay Sands Suites",
    "street_line2": "21-37",
    "province_state": "Singapore"
  },
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "PHYSICAL_PRODUCT",
      "name": "Dyson Vacuum",
      "net_unit_amount": 10000,
      "quantity": 1
    }
  ],

  "updated": "2021-12-31T11:59:59Z"

Expand All
object

Payment request object

business_id
string

Xendit-generated identifier for the business that owns the transaction

Example5f27a14a9bf05c73dd040bc8
reference_id
string

A Reference ID from merchants to identify their request.

Min length1
Max length64
payment_request_id
string

Xendit unique Payment Request ID generated as reference after creation of payment request.

Examplepr-1102feb0-bb79-47ae-9d1e-e69394d3949c
payment_token_id
string

Xendit unique Payment Token ID generated as reference for reusable payment details of the end user.

Examplept-cc3938dc-c2a5-43c4-89d7-7570793348c2
customer_id
string

Xendit unique Capture ID generated as reference for the end user

Min length41
Max length41
Examplecust-b98d6f63-d240-44ec-9bd5-aa42954c4f48
latest_payment_id
string

Latest Payment ID linked to the payment request.

Examplepy-1402feb0-bb79-47ae-9d1e-e69394d3949c
type
string

The payment collection intent type for the payment request. SINGLE_PAYMENT: receive a single payment for the request. multiple captures are encapsulated as a single payment. individual recurring payments counts as a single payment each. PAY_AND_SAVE: receive a single payment for the request. if the payment is successful, a payment token will be returned for subsequent use. MULTIPLE_PAYMENTS: receive multiple payments for the request. only used for fixed payment instruments like static QR or fixed VAs etc.

Valid values[ "SINGLE_PAYMENT", "PAY_AND_SAVE", "MULTIPLE_PAYMENTS" ]
country
string

ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 two-letter country code for the country of transaction.

Valid values[ "ID", "PH", "VN", "TH", "SG", "MY" ]
ExampleID
currency
string

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment.

Valid values[ "IDR", "PHP", "VND", "THB", "SGD", "MYR", "USD" ]
ExampleIDR
request_amount
number

The intended payment amount to be collected from the end user.

Minimum0
Example10000
capture_method
string

AUTOMATIC: payment capture will be processed immediately after payment request is created. MANUAL: payment capture requires merchant's trigger via payment capture endpoint before being processed

Valid values[ "AUTOMATIC", "MANUAL" ]
Default"AUTOMATIC"
ExampleAUTOMATIC
channel_code
string
channel_properties
OneOf
ChannelPropertiesCards
mid_label
string

For Switcher Merchants only. Specify the MID label to route the payment.

statement_descriptor
string

Short descriptor that can be used as an identifier on the end user card statement. Subjected to support by issuers.

Min length41
Max length41
skip_three_ds
boolean

Specify if 3DS should be skipped for the payment.

Defaultfalse
success_return_url
string

URL where the end-customer is redirected if the linking is successful. This is required for skip 3DS transactions to handle soft declines.

Min length41
Max length41
failure_return_url
string

URL where the end-customer is redirected if the linking has failed. This is required for skip 3DS transactions to handle soft declines.

Min length41
Max length41
card_on_file_type
string

Required when creating payment tokens or when payment request type is PAY_AND_SAVE:. The type of “credential-on-file” / “card-on-file” / COF for future payments.

If you intend to use the card details for future COF transactions, you must be accurately indicate the card_on_file_type to allow Xendit to optimize the payment setup with the processor, which will help all future transactions using the same card details.

CUSTOMER_UNSCHEDULED: for future payments initiated by the customer that do not follow a schedule. For example, a simple “save card for future checkout” eCommerce flow, where the customer can use the saved card for future purchases any time.

MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED: for future payments initiated without customer interaction and do not follow a schedule. For example, an auto top-up payment flow, where an application automatically collects payment when customer's balance is low.

RECURRING: for future payments initiated at fixed and regular intervals. For example, a subscription payment flow, where the customer is billed automatically every month.

To process MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED and RECURRING payments, there are some additional configuration required. For Aggregator Merchants, you need to be allowed to perform transaction without 3DS, please check our guides to enable this, or contact a Xendit representative for help. For Switcher Merchants, please check with your acquiring bank whether your MID allows non 3DS transactions and support recurring transactions by the acquirer.

Valid values[ "CUSTOMER_UNSCHEDULED", "MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED", "RECURRING" ]

Server Side Error

Title: Validate a payment request using OTP
Content:
Post
/payment_requests/{payment_request_id}/validate_otp

Submit OTP to validate the payment request that was created. In supported payment channels with flows that require OTP, merchants have the option to submit the OTP via API. Merchants should build a page to receive OTP input from end user when they receive the OTP notification.

Security
HTTP
Type basic
Path parameters
payment_request_id
stringRequired
Min length39
Max length39
Examplepr-8877c08a-740d-4153-9816-3d744ed197a5
Body parameters
otp_code
string

OTP code used to verify the transaction with payment method provider.

Example123456
Responses
200

Validate OTP for a Payment Request

validateOtpPaymentResponseRedirect

string

Examplepy-1402feb0-bb79-47ae-9d1e-e69394d3949c
type
string

The payment collection intent type for the payment request. SINGLE_PAYMENT: receive a single payment for the request. multiple captures are encapsulated as a single payment. individual recurring payments counts as a single payment each. PAY_AND_SAVE: receive a single payment for the request. if the payment is successful, a payment token will be returned for subsequent use. MULTIPLE_PAYMENTS: receive multiple payments for the request. only used for fixed payment instruments like static QR or fixed VAs etc.

Valid values[ "SINGLE_PAYMENT", "PAY_AND_SAVE", "MULTIPLE_PAYMENTS" ]
country
string

ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 two-letter country code for the country of transaction.

Valid values[ "ID", "PH", "VN", "TH", "SG", "MY" ]
ExampleID
currency
string

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment.

Valid values[ "IDR", "PHP", "VND", "THB", "SGD", "MYR", "USD" ]
ExampleIDR
request_amount
number

The intended payment amount to be collected from the end user.

Minimum0
Example10000
capture_method
string

AUTOMATIC: payment capture will be processed immediately after payment request is created. MANUAL: payment capture requires merchant's trigger via payment capture endpoint before being processed

Valid values[ "AUTOMATIC", "MANUAL" ]
Default"AUTOMATIC"
ExampleAUTOMATIC

failure_return_url
string

Min length41
Max length41
card_on_file_type
string

Required when creating payment tokens or when payment request type is PAY_AND_SAVE:. The type of “credential-on-file” / “card-on-file” / COF for future payments.

If you intend to use the card details for future COF transactions, you must be accurately indicate the card_on_file_type to allow Xendit to optimize the payment setup with the processor, which will help all future transactions using the same card details.

CUSTOMER_UNSCHEDULED: for future payments initiated by the customer that do not follow a schedule. For example, a simple “save card for future checkout” eCommerce flow, where the customer can use the saved card for future purchases any time.

MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED: for future payments initiated without customer interaction and do not follow a schedule. For example, an auto top-up payment flow, where an application automatically collects payment when customer's balance is low.

RECURRING: for future payments initiated at fixed and regular intervals. For example, a subscription payment flow, where the customer is billed automatically every month.

To process MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED and RECURRING payments, there are some additional configuration required. For Aggregator Merchants, you need to be allowed to perform transaction without 3DS, please check our guides to enable this, or contact a Xendit representative for help. For Switcher Merchants, please check with your acquiring bank whether your MID allows non 3DS transactions and support recurring transactions by the acquirer.

Valid values[ "CUSTOMER_UNSCHEDULED", "MERCHANT_UNSCHEDULED", "RECURRING" ]

authentication_value
string

The result value from the 3DS transaction received from the ACS. This value is no longer present on responses after 45 days have passed after the authentication. Note that Mastercard and Visa use a different underlying format. This property will only be present in the ARes if transStatus is Y, A and conditionally (by DS rules) I.
